content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Magic Stick". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1257/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case contains an integer n, the length of the array. Then n integers a1, a2, ..., an, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of operations required to make all elements of the array equal.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the array and then find the median. The minimum number of operations is the sum of the absolute differences between each element and the median.

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(input())):
    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    arr.sort()
    median = arr[n // 2]
    sum = 0
    for i in range(n):
        sum += abs(arr[i] - median)
    print(sum)
